'''Chris Tuson''' (born 25 February 1988) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}}. He spent most of his career at the [[Wigan Warriors]], but also played for the [[Blackpool Panthers]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Hull F.C.]]

==Background==
Born in [[Leyland, Lancashire|Leyland]], [[Lancashire]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Tuson’s ready and willing|url=http://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors/tuson-s-ready-and-willing-1-3753115|website=Wigan Today|accessdate=2 August 2014|date=8 September 2011}}</ref> Tuson started his career with amateur clubs Leyland Warriors and Bamber Bridge,<ref>{{cite web|last1=Kelly|first1=Robert|title=Warrior Tuson ready for battle|url=http://www.leylandguardian.co.uk/sport/other-sports/warrior-tuson-ready-for-battle-1-2951319|website=Leyland Guardian|accessdate=2 August 2014|date=21 January 2011}}</ref> and attended [[Balshaw's CE High School]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Agony almost over for Chris|url=http://www.leylandguardian.co.uk/sport/agony-almost-over-for-chris-1-2011565|website=Leyland Guardian|accessdate=2 August 2014|date=24 December 2008}}</ref>

==Career==
He was signed by [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2006,<ref>{{cite web|title=Chris Tuson signs new Wigan Warriors contract|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/9254268.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=2 August 2014|date=3 December 2010}}</ref> and made his first team début from the bench against [[Leeds Rhinos]] in 2008.

In 2010, Tuson made his second appearance for Wigan against [[Castleford Tigers]], and also made one appearance for [[Blackpool Panthers]] as a dual registration player before joining Castleford on loan for one month.<ref>{{cite web|title=Tuson Joins Tigers On Loan|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=4611&type=1|publisher=Wigan Warriors|accessdate=2 August 2014|date=24 March 2010|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140815041635/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=4611&type=1|archivedate=15 August 2014}}</ref> The loan deal was later extended,<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan Warrior Chris Tuson extends Castleford stay|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/8647598.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=2 August 2014|date=27 April 2010}}</ref> and Tuson went on to appear eight times for the club.

In June 2013, he rejected a new contract from Wigan and agreed to join [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] for the start of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Chris Tuson: Wigan Warriors forward to join Wakefield Wildcats|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22766624|work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 February 2014|date=4 June 2013}}</ref> However, the move failed to materialise due to Wakefield's financial problems, and Tuson opted to join [[Hull F.C.]] instead.<ref>{{cite web|title=Chris Tuson: Hull FC sign Wigan back row for next season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24349192|work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 February 2014|date=1 October 2013}}</ref>

He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= Guardian|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In July 2014, Tuson announced his retirement due to a recurring back injury.<ref>{{cite web|title=Chris Tuson: Hull FC forward forced to retire through injury|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28551755|website=BBC Sport|access-date=2 August 2014|date=29 July 2014}}</ref>

In 2023, Tuson began working an office job in the football industry. He works with prior Wigan Warriors Player Phil Clarke, Luge Pairs (2026 Milan) Gold Medalist Jamie Roberts, and former Fat Italian chef Johnnie "Two N's" Martin-Scott.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Club DNA |url=https://club-dna.com/ |access-date=2026-02-16 |website=clubdna |language=en-US}}</ref>
